We are a regional electric cooperative serving 380,000 members. Our biggest challenge is unplanned outages — transformers fail without warning, causing extended downtime and expensive emergency repairs. We want AI to predict equipment failures before they happen.

questions.a: Regional electric cooperative serving 380,000 residential and commercial members across 4 counties with 75,000+ meters.
Unplanned transformer failures cause 6-12 hour outages. We replace equipment reactively instead of predictively. Each major failure costs $50K-$200K in emergency repairs.
Grid Operations, Field Services

agents.name: AI Control Tower
agents.role: Central orchestrator monitoring all grid sensors, coordinating predictive maintenance, and generating board-ready reliability reports

agents.name: Outage Predictor
agents.role: Analyzes transformer thermal data, load patterns, and weather to predict failures 48 hours in advance

agents.name: Grid Monitor
agents.role: Continuously monitors voltage, frequency, and power quality across all feeders and substations in real-time

agents.name: Sensor Analyst
agents.role: Processes IoT sensor data from smart meters, line sensors, and substation monitors to detect degradation patterns

agents.name: Maintenance Scheduler
agents.role: Auto-schedules preventive maintenance crews based on failure predictions, minimizing member impact

agents.name: Parts Forecaster
agents.role: Predicts spare parts needs based on failure probabilities and maintains optimal inventory levels

agents.name: Asset Inspector
agents.role: Prioritizes physical inspections by AI-ranked risk scores instead of fixed rotation schedules

agents.name: Member Notifier
agents.role: Proactively alerts members about planned maintenance and provides real-time restoration ETAs

agents.name: Cost Tracker
agents.role: Tracks savings from prevented outages and calculates ROI of predictive maintenance investments

agents.name: Reliability Reporter
agents.role: Auto-generates SAIDI/SAIFI reliability reports and tracks improvement trends for regulatory filings

sim.action: Scanning 75,000 meters and 2,400 transformers for anomalies
sim.narr: The AI Control Tower monitors every piece of equipment on your grid.

sim.action: Alert: Transformer T-4821 thermal stress — 87% failure probability within 48 hours
sim.narr: AI catches the failure pattern days before it would cause an outage.

sim.action: Confirmed: Feeder 7 load imbalance correlating with T-4821 degradation
sim.narr: Grid monitoring validates the prediction with real-time power data.

sim.action: Auto-scheduled preventive maintenance — zero member impact
sim.narr: Preventive maintenance instead of emergency repair. No outage.

sim.action: Monthly: 14 failures predicted, 13 prevented, $185K avoided
sim.narr: AI turns reactive grid management into proactive operations.

sim.action: Year-to-date: 92% fewer unplanned outages
sim.narr: Members experience fewer outages. Board sees measurable results.

narr.idea: Imagine your cooperative could predict equipment failures before they happen...
narr.questions: The AI advisor learns about your grid operations...
narr.design: AI recommends grid intelligence and predictive monitoring...
narr.results: Your AI system: 3 agents, $185K savings, 92% fewer unplanned outages.
narr.sim: Watch AI predict and prevent a transformer failure.

idea: We serve 380,000 members and during storms our call center gets overwhelmed with 15,000+ calls. Members wait 45+ minutes for updates. Crew dispatch is manual and slow. We need AI to handle storm communication and coordinate restoration.

questions.a: Regional electric cooperative. During severe weather, we handle 15,000+ inbound calls and coordinate 200+ field crews.
Call center overwhelmed — 15,000 calls in 3 hours. Members angry about long hold times. Manual crew dispatch takes hours.
Customer Comms, Crew Dispatch, Restoration Planning

agents.name: AI Control Tower

agents.name: Storm Response Bot

agents.name: Social Media Monitor

agents.name: Crew Dispatcher

agents.name: Damage Assessor

agents.name: Mutual Aid Coordinator
agents.role: Identifies when mutual aid crews are needed and coordinates with neighboring co-ops automatically

agents.name: Grid Rerouter

agents.name: Weather Tracker

agents.name: Storm Cost Analyst

sim.action: Storm alert: Category 2 approaching. 42,000 members in projected path.

sim.action: Auto-sent alerts to 42,000 members via text and email
sim.narr: Members notified before they lose power — reducing panic calls.

sim.action: Pre-positioned 12 crews at staging areas closest to impact zones
sim.narr: AI pre-stages crews for fastest possible response time.

sim.action: Handling 8,400 inbound calls simultaneously with real-time ETAs
sim.narr: No hold times. Every member gets a personalized restoration estimate.

sim.action: Restoration 62% complete. Rerouting 3 crews to critical areas.
sim.narr: The Control Tower continuously optimizes crew allocation.

sim.action: Result: 60% fewer inbound calls vs. comparable storm last year
sim.narr: Proactive communication dramatically reduces call center load.

narr.idea: Imagine handling a major storm without your call center being overwhelmed...
narr.questions: The AI advisor learns about your storm response...

idea: We have 75,000+ meters but limited real-time visibility. Theft detection takes months. Meter malfunctions go undetected. We want AI to analyze meter data for anomalies, theft, and demand patterns.

questions.a: Regional electric cooperative with 75,000+ AMI meters needing better real-time visibility into usage, theft, and equipment health.
Theft costs $180K+ annually. Meter malfunctions go undetected for weeks. Can't forecast demand accurately.
Theft Detection, Anomaly Alerts

agents.name: AI Control Tower
agents.role: Monitors all 75,000 meters and coordinates between theft detection, maintenance, and billing teams

agents.name: Meter Analyzer
agents.role: Detects usage anomalies, theft patterns, and meter malfunctions across the entire fleet in real-time

agents.name: Theft Investigator
agents.role: Identifies suspected energy theft by analyzing consumption drops, bypass signatures, and tamper alerts

agents.name: Demand Forecaster
agents.role: Predicts peak demand periods, seasonal shifts, and growth trends from historical and real-time meter data

agents.name: Meter Health Monitor
agents.role: Tracks communication health of all AMI meters and flags units needing replacement or repair

agents.name: Field Dispatch
agents.role: Auto-dispatches technicians for meter investigations, replacements, and tamper inspections

agents.name: Billing Validator
agents.role: Cross-references meter readings with billing records to catch discrepancies before invoices go out

agents.name: Revenue Recovery Agent
agents.role: Calculates back-billing amounts for confirmed theft cases and tracks recovery progress

agents.name: Member Alert Bot
agents.role: Notifies members of unusual usage spikes that may indicate leaks, malfunctions, or unauthorized use

sim.action: Real-time scan: 75,842 meters active. 23 anomalies flagged.
sim.narr: Every meter monitored continuously.

sim.action: Theft detected: Account #34821 showing 40% consumption drop
sim.narr: AI catches theft patterns that take months manually.

idea: We spend $4M a year on vegetation management and everyone knows it is not optimized. We trim on a fixed 3-year cycle regardless of actual risk. High-risk corridors grow unchecked while we waste money trimming low-risk areas. Vegetation causes 35% of our outages. We need AI to tell us where to trim and where to skip so we cut unnecessary spending and actually reduce outages.

questions.a: Regional electric cooperative with 4,200 miles of distribution lines. Vegetation causes 35% of our outages. We spend $4M/year on trimming.

questions.q: How do you manage vegetation now?
questions.a: Combination

questions.q: What is your biggest vegetation challenge?
questions.a: We trim every 3 years regardless of risk. Crews trim low-risk corridors while high-risk areas grow into lines. No way to prioritize. $4M budget with no visibility into where it actually matters.

questions.q: What would save the most money?
questions.a: Risk-Based Scheduling, Eliminate Low-Value Trimming

agents.name: AI Control Tower
agents.role: Correlates vegetation data with outage history and weather to identify highest-risk corridors across the territory

agents.name: Vegetation Analyzer
agents.role: Analyzes satellite imagery, LiDAR data, and growth models to prioritize trimming by actual risk

agents.name: Growth Predictor
agents.role: Forecasts vegetation growth rates by species, season, and location to schedule trimming before encroachment

agents.name: Outage Correlator
agents.role: Maps historical vegetation-caused outages to specific corridors, species, and weather conditions

agents.name: Crew Scheduler
agents.role: Optimizes trimming crew routes and schedules based on AI risk rankings and access constraints

agents.name: Contractor Manager
agents.role: Tracks contractor progress, quality scores, and compliance with right-of-way specifications

agents.name: Inspection Drone
agents.role: Coordinates drone flights for visual inspection of flagged high-risk corridors between trimming cycles

agents.name: Budget Optimizer
agents.role: Allocates vegetation management budget to maximize outage reduction per dollar spent

agents.name: Easement Tracker
agents.role: Monitors right-of-way easement compliance and flags properties requiring access negotiations

sim.action: Analyzing 4,200 miles of corridors. Correlating trim history, outage data, and growth patterns.
sim.narr: AI combines every data source you have to build a risk map of your entire territory.

sim.action: High-risk alert: Corridor 7-B has 18-inch encroachment. 73% outage probability in 60 days.
sim.narr: Risk-based prioritization. Trim what actually matters, not what is next on the 3-year schedule.

sim.action: Low-value flag: Corridor 12-A scheduled for trim but risk score is 8/100. Skip it this cycle.
sim.narr: This is where the real savings come from. Stop trimming corridors that do not need it.

sim.action: Rescheduled: 12-A deferred, 7-B and 9-C moved to this week. Saving $34K this month.
sim.narr: Same crews, same budget. Just pointed at the right corridors.

sim.action: Quarter projection: $180K saved from deferred low-risk trimming. Zero increase in veg outages.
sim.narr: The board sees savings. Members see fewer outages. Everyone wins.

sim.action: Annual result: 10-20% trimming cost reduction. Vegetation outages down 35%. $400K-$800K saved.
sim.narr: On a $4M vegetation budget, that is real money. And it is immediately measurable.

narr.idea: Imagine cutting your trimming spend 10-20% while actually reducing vegetation-caused outages...
narr.questions: The AI advisor learns about your vegetation management challenges...
narr.design: AI recommends risk-based scheduling and spend optimization...
narr.results: Your Smart Vegetation System: 9 agents, 10-20% cost reduction, 35% fewer veg outages.
narr.sim: Watch AI tell your crews where to trim, where to skip, and where the money is being wasted.

idea: Rate case preparation takes 4-6 months of manual work. Cost-of-service studies require data from 12 systems. We want AI to automate regulatory filing preparation.

questions.a: Regional electric cooperative filing rate cases with the state PUC every 3 years. Each filing takes 4-6 months.
Every 3 years
Data in 12 systems. Cost-of-service studies are manual Excel work. PUC formatting is error-prone. Team of 3 people.
Data Aggregation, Cost Analysis, Filing Generation

agents.name: AI Control Tower

agents.name: Rate Case Analyst

agents.name: Data Integrator

agents.name: Filing Generator

agents.name: Depreciation Calculator

agents.name: Load Study Analyst

agents.name: Testimony Drafter
agents.role: Generates first drafts of expert testimony and discovery responses based on filing data

agents.name: Benchmark Agent

agents.name: Member Impact Modeler

sim.action: Pulled 847,000 records from billing, accounting, engineering, ops
sim.narr: Data from 12 systems reconciled automatically.

sim.action: Cost-of-service complete: revenue requirement $47.2M, 3.8% adjustment
sim.narr: 6 weeks of manual analysis done in hours.

sim.action: Generated PUC-compliant filing with all schedules and exhibits
sim.narr: Formatting matches regulatory requirements exactly.

sim.action: Review: 0 data errors, 100% compliance with filing requirements
sim.narr: AI catches errors before submission.

sim.action: Result: Filed in 3 weeks instead of 4 months
sim.narr: 80% faster. Team focuses on strategy, not data entry.

narr.idea: Imagine preparing your next rate case in weeks instead of months...
narr.questions: The AI advisor learns about your regulatory process...
narr.design: AI recommends regulatory automation and data integration...
narr.results: Your AI system: 3 agents, 80% faster prep, zero errors.
narr.sim: Watch AI prepare a complete rate case from 12 data sources.

idea: We are a mid-size electric cooperative with 200+ field crew members and 85 service vehicles. Our biggest problem is productivity. Supervisors spend 2 hours each morning on the phone assigning jobs. Crews drive long distances for low-value work. We have no way to prioritize which jobs matter most. We need AI to generate daily work plans, optimize routes, and get 10-15% more work done without adding headcount.

questions.a: Mid-size electric cooperative with 200+ field crew, 85 vehicles. Our crews cover 4,200 miles of distribution line across 4 counties.

questions.q: How large is your field ops team?
What is your biggest field ops challenge?
questions.a: Supervisors spend 2+ hours each morning calling crews to assign jobs. Crews waste time driving to low-value work while high-priority jobs wait. No prioritized daily plan. Multiple truck rolls to the same area in the same week.

questions.q: What would make the biggest impact?
questions.a: Daily Work Plans, Route Optimization, Fewer Truck Rolls

agents.name: AI Control Tower
agents.role: Coordinates all field operations, prioritizes jobs by severity and member impact, and tracks fleet-wide KPIs

agents.name: Crew Dispatcher
agents.role: Auto-assigns crews to work orders based on location, skills, certifications, availability, and job priority

agents.name: Route Optimizer
agents.role: Calculates optimal driving routes across all active assignments, reducing windshield time by 28%

agents.name: Emergency Coordinator
agents.role: Detects emergency work orders and immediately reroutes the nearest qualified crew, reassigning their non-urgent work

agents.name: Vehicle Tracker
agents.role: Monitors real-time GPS location, speed, and idle time for all 85 fleet vehicles

agents.name: Preventive Scheduler
agents.role: Schedules vehicle maintenance, DOT inspections, and equipment calibrations based on usage patterns

agents.name: Timecard Validator
agents.role: Validates crew time entries against GPS data, job completions, and union work rules for accurate payroll

agents.name: Fuel Analyst
agents.role: Tracks fuel consumption by vehicle and route, identifies inefficient driving patterns, and projects monthly fuel costs

agents.name: Safety Monitor
agents.role: Monitors driving behavior, job site safety compliance, and training certification expiration dates

sim.action: Morning scan: 47 work orders, 85 vehicles, 200 crew members. Generating daily plan.
sim.narr: AI sees every job and every crew simultaneously. No phone calls needed.

sim.action: Generated prioritized daily plan: 12 highest-value jobs assigned to 32 crews in 8 seconds
sim.narr: What took supervisors 2 hours of phone calls done in 8 seconds. Prioritized by impact.

sim.action: Optimized all 32 routes. Eliminated 3 duplicate truck rolls. Saving 340 miles today.
sim.narr: Crews clustered geographically. No more driving across the county for a single low-value job.

sim.action: Flagged: 4 low-value jobs deprioritized. "Don't bother going here today."
sim.narr: AI tells crews what NOT to do. That is where the real savings come from.

sim.action: Emergency: Downed line on Highway 7. Nearest qualified crew rerouted. ETA 14 minutes.
sim.narr: Emergency response in minutes. Non-urgent work automatically reassigned.

sim.action: Weekly result: 15% more line covered per crew. $28K saved in reduced truck rolls this week.
sim.narr: Same headcount, dramatically more work done. That is $1.5M annualized on a $15M field ops budget.

narr.idea: Imagine every crew getting a prioritized list of the 12 highest-value jobs each morning...
narr.questions: The AI advisor learns about your field operations challenges...
narr.design: AI recommends crew productivity and route optimization engines...
narr.results: Your Crew Productivity Engine: 9 agents, 15% more line per crew, $1.5M in savings.
narr.sim: Watch AI eliminate wasted truck rolls and optimize your field operations.

idea: NERC, FERC, and state PUC compliance consumes one full-time employee. Reports are manual, error-prone, and stressful before deadlines. We want AI to auto-generate reports from operational data.

questions.a: Regional cooperative subject to NERC reliability standards, FERC reporting, and state PUC oversight.
One FTE assembles reports manually from 8 systems. Audit errors are embarrassing. Deadline pressure is constant.
Auto Reporting, Continuous Monitoring

agents.name: Environmental Reporter

agents.name: Policy Updater
